questions.question: What percentage of failed sales hires fail because of poor onboarding, not poor hiring?
questions.explanation: 60% of failed sales hires fail because of poor onboarding. SDRs with structured onboarding ramp 50% faster. The first 30 days determine the first 90 days, and the first 90 days determine whether the hire works at all.

questions.question: What should a new sales hire do in their first 3 days of onboarding?

options.text: Listen to a library of 10+ recorded discovery calls
Set up their CRM from scratch

questions.explanation: The first 3 days should be spent listening to recorded calls — 10 discovery calls and 5 closing calls minimum. Your sales motion works because of the way you say things, and that doesn't live in a document. It lives in recordings.

questions.question: What is the Day 30 bar for your new hire?

options.text: Independently sending sequences, handling first 10 min of discovery, and fielding basic objections
Being able to present at a conference

questions.explanation: By Day 30, your hire should independently send outreach sequences, handle the first 10 minutes of a discovery call, and field basic objections. Not quota attainment. Not closing deals. Independence on the activities they own.

questions.question: 'Just read the playbook' is sufficient onboarding for a new sales hire.
questions.explanation: False. Documentation alone isn't enough. Your sales motion works because of timing, phrasing, and instinct that only live in actual call recordings. Build a call library and make listening to it the first 3 days of onboarding.

questions.question: Do you have a library of recorded sales calls? If not, what calls would you record this month? Design a Week 1 onboarding agenda for your first sales hire.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response assesses their current call library (or plans to build one), designs a realistic Week 1 agenda with specific activities per day, and focuses on voice transfer and immersion before any outreach activity begins.
